article_context.source_excerpt:
# Balcony Composting Guide

Balcony composting turns kitchen scraps into potting gold without smells or pests, using a sealed bokashi bin or a compact worm farm sized for small spaces. Both methods fit under a bench and process a household's vegetable scraps.

Bokashi ferments scraps with bran in an airtight bucket. Worm farms digest scraps into castings. Both need a carbon balance and weekly attention.

Start with fruit and vegetable peels, avoid meat and dairy in worm farms, and drain bokashi tea twice a week to use as diluted fertilizer.
